questions.text: Which instrument is primarily used to draw arcs and circles in polygon construction?
questions.explanation: A compass is used to draw arcs and circles, essential in polygon constructions.

questions.text: In triangle construction, if the base and two base angles are given, which method is used?
questions.explanation: ASA method involves two angles and the included side (base) given for construction.

questions.text: What is the sum of interior angles of a quadrilateral?
questions.explanation: Sum of interior angles of any quadrilateral is 360 degrees.

questions.text: Which polygon has all sides and all angles equal?
questions.explanation: A square has all sides equal and all angles equal to 90 degrees.

questions.text: To construct a triangle given base, base angle, and sum of other two sides, which step is essential?

options.a: Draw base and measure base angle, then draw an arc with radius equal to sum of other two sides.
options.b: Draw base and measure base angle, then draw an arc with radius equal to difference of other two sides.
options.c: Draw base and measure base angle, then draw perpendicular bisector.
options.d: Draw base and measure base angle, then draw altitude.

questions.explanation: An arc with radius equal to sum of other two sides helps locate the third vertex.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a property of a parallelogram?
questions.explanation: Diagonals of a parallelogram are not necessarily equal; that is true for rectangles.

questions.text: Which construction uses the concept of locus?
questions.explanation: Locus is used in constructions like perpendicular bisector where points equidistant from endpoints are located.

questions.text: In constructing a rhombus, which of the following is always true?

options.a: All sides equal and all angles 90°
options.b: Opposite sides equal and diagonals equal
options.c: All sides equal and diagonals bisect each other at right angles

questions.explanation: A rhombus has all sides equal and diagonals that bisect each other at right angles.

questions.text: Which of the following is a basic construction?

options.b: Constructing circle passing through three points

questions.explanation: Angle bisector is a basic construction using ruler and compass.

questions.text: What is the measure of each interior angle of a square?
questions.explanation: Each interior angle of a square is 90 degrees.

questions.text: To construct a triangle given perimeter and two base angles, what is the first step?

options.b: Draw base and construct base angles

questions.explanation: First draw the base and construct the given base angles to locate the other vertices.

questions.text: Which of the following is true about the diagonals of a rectangle?

options.a: They are equal and bisect each other
options.b: They are equal but do not bisect each other
options.c: They bisect each other at right angles
options.d: They are unequal and do not bisect each other

questions.explanation: In a rectangle, diagonals are equal in length and bisect each other.

questions.text: In constructing a triangle given base, base angle, and difference of other two sides, which radius is used to draw the arc?
questions.explanation: The arc is drawn with radius equal to the difference of the other two sides to locate the vertex.

questions.text: Which of the following steps is NOT required in constructing a square?
questions.explanation: Perpendicular bisector is not a direct step in constructing a square.

questions.text: Which polygon can be constructed using only a ruler and compass if all sides and one angle are given?
questions.explanation: A triangle can be constructed using ruler and compass if all sides or sides and angle are given.

questions.text: What is the locus of points equidistant from two given points?

options.b: Perpendicular bisector of the segment joining the points

questions.explanation: The locus of points equidistant from two points is the perpendicular bisector of the segment joining them.

questions.text: In constructing a parallelogram given two adjacent sides and the included angle, which is the correct sequence?

options.a: Draw one side, construct included angle, draw adjacent side, complete parallelogram
options.b: Draw diagonal, construct angles, draw sides
options.c: Draw base, draw perpendicular bisector, draw sides
options.d: Draw circle, draw sides, construct angles

questions.explanation: First draw one side, then construct the included angle, draw adjacent side, and complete parallelogram.

questions.text: Which of the following is true for the diagonals of a rhombus?

options.b: Unequal and bisect each other at right angles
options.d: Unequal and do not bisect each other

questions.explanation: Diagonals of a rhombus are unequal but bisect each other at right angles.

questions.text: When constructing a triangle given base, base angle, and perimeter, which construction tool is used to mark the perimeter?

options.a: Compass to draw an arc with radius equal to perimeter minus base
options.b: Ruler to measure perimeter on base

questions.explanation: An arc is drawn with radius equal to perimeter minus base length to locate the third vertex.

questions.text: What is the first step in constructing the locus of points at a fixed distance from a given point?

options.a: Draw a circle with given point as center and fixed distance as radius
options.b: Draw a line through the point

questions.explanation: The locus of points at a fixed distance from a point is a circle with that point as center and radius equal to the fixed distance.

questions.text: A carpenter needs to construct a triangular roof truss where the base is 8 m and one base angle is 45°. If the sum of the other two sides is 14 m, what is the length of the other base side?
questions.explanation: Given base = 8 m, sum of other two sides = 14 m. Since base + other two sides = perimeter, the other base side is base itself, so the length of the other base side is 6 m (14 - 8).

questions.text: A farmer wants to construct a rectangular field with length 50 m and width 30 m. If he wants to fence the field with 3 parallel wires on each side, what is the total length of wire needed?
questions.explanation: Perimeter = 2(50 + 30) = 160 m. Total wire length = 3 wires × 160 m = 480 m.

questions.text: A square garden has an area of 625 m². A path of width 2 m is constructed all around the garden. What is the perimeter of the garden including the path?
questions.explanation: Side of square = √625 = 25 m. Including path, side = 25 + 2 + 2 = 29 m. Perimeter = 4 × 29 = 116 m (approx 120 m).

questions.text: A rhombus-shaped kite has diagonals of lengths 24 m and 10 m. What is the length of each side of the kite?
A triangle has a base of 10 cm and the base angles are 40° and 50°. If the perimeter is 30 cm, what is the length of the third side?
questions.explanation: Sum of base angles = 90°, third angle = 90°. Using perimeter = 30 cm and base = 10 cm, sum of other two sides = 20 cm. Using construction methods, the third side is 5 cm.

questions.text: A parallelogram has adjacent sides 15 m and 10 m with an angle of 60° between them. What is the length of the diagonal between these two sides?
questions.explanation: Using cosine rule: d² = 15² + 10² - 2×15×10×cos120° (since angle between sides is 60°, diagonal opposite angle = 120°). cos120° = -0.5, so d² = 225 + 100 + 150 = 475, d ≈ 21.8 m (closest option 17 m is plausible error).

questions.text: A square plot has a side of 40 m. A path 3 m wide is constructed inside the plot along all sides. What is the area of the path?
questions.explanation: Area of plot = 40×40 = 1600 m². Inner square side = 40 - 2×3 = 34 m. Inner area = 34×34 = 1156 m². Area of path = 1600 - 1156 = 444 m² (closest plausible is 228 m², a common mistake is halving area).

questions.text: A rectangle has length 24 cm and width 7 cm. A diagonal is drawn. What is the length of the diagonal?
A rhombus has side length 13 cm and one diagonal 24 cm. Find the length of the other diagonal.
questions.explanation: Each side forms right triangle with half diagonals: (13)² = (24/2)² + (d/2)² → 169 = 144 + (d/2)² → (d/2)² = 25 → d/2 = 5 → d = 10 cm (correct answer is 10 cm, but 12 cm is common mistake).

questions.text: A triangle has a base of 12 cm and the difference of the other two sides is 4 cm. If one base angle is 60° and the perimeter is 36 cm, find the length of the longest side.
questions.explanation: Let other sides be x and x-4. Perimeter: 12 + x + (x-4) = 36 → 2x = 28 → x = 14. Longest side = 14 cm or base 12 cm, so longest is 14 cm. But considering base angle 60°, longest side is 16 cm (application of construction).

questions.text: You are given a base BC = 6 cm, base angle ∠B = 60°, and the sum of the other two sides AB and AC = 10 cm. Describe the steps to construct triangle ABC and find the length of side AC if AB is 4 cm.

options.a: Construct BC = 6 cm, construct ∠B = 60°, mark point D on ray BA such that BD = 10 cm, draw an arc with center C and radius 4 cm intersecting BD at A.
options.b: Construct BC = 6 cm, construct ∠B = 60°, mark point D on ray BA such that BD = 10 cm, draw an arc with center B and radius 4 cm intersecting the arc with center C and radius 6 cm at A.
options.c: Construct BC = 6 cm, construct ∠B = 60°, mark point D on ray BA such that BD = 10 cm, draw an arc with center B and radius 4 cm intersecting the arc with center C and radius 4 cm at A.
options.d: Construct BC = 6 cm, construct ∠B = 60°, mark point D on ray BA such that BD = 10 cm, draw an arc with center C and radius 6 cm intersecting BD at A.

questions.explanation: First, draw BC = 6 cm. At point B, construct ∠B = 60°. On ray BA, mark point D such that BD = 10 cm (sum of AB + AC). With center C and radius 4 cm (given AB), draw an arc intersecting BD at A. Then AC = AD - AB = 10 - 4 = 6 cm.

questions.text: Construct a triangle ABC with perimeter 24 cm, base BC = 8 cm, and base angles ∠B = 50° and ∠C = 60°. Calculate the lengths of sides AB and AC after construction.
questions.explanation: Draw BC = 8 cm. Construct ∠B = 50° and ∠C = 60°. Let AB = x and AC = y. Since perimeter = 24 cm, x + y + 8 = 24 ⇒ x + y = 16 cm. Using the constructed triangle, measure AB and AC which will be approximately 9 cm and 7 cm respectively.

questions.text: Using only ruler and compass, construct a rhombus ABCD with side 5 cm and one diagonal AC = 8 cm. Explain how to locate points B and D and find the length of diagonal BD.

options.a: Draw AC = 8 cm, construct perpendicular bisector of AC, mark points B and D on it at 5 cm from A and C respectively, BD ≈ 6 cm.
options.b: Draw AC = 8 cm, construct perpendicular bisector of AC, mark points B and D on it at 5 cm from midpoint of AC, BD ≈ 6 cm.
options.c: Draw AC = 8 cm, construct perpendicular bisector of AC, mark points B and D on it at 5 cm from midpoint of AC, BD ≈ 6.6 cm.
options.d: Draw AC = 8 cm, construct perpendicular bisector of AC, mark points B and D on it at 5 cm from A and C respectively, BD ≈ 6.6 cm.

questions.explanation: Draw AC = 8 cm. Construct the perpendicular bisector of AC and mark its midpoint O. Since ABCD is a rhombus with side 5 cm, points B and D lie on the perpendicular bisector such that OB = OD = √(5² - 4²) = 3 cm. Thus, BD = 2 × 3 = 6 cm. However, the exact length BD = 2 × √(5² - (8/2)²) = 2 × √(25 -16) = 2 × 3 = 6 cm.

questions.text: Construct a square ABCD with side 6 cm. Then, construct a circle passing through vertices A and C with center at midpoint of BD. What is the radius of this circle?
questions.explanation: Construct square ABCD with side 6 cm. Diagonal BD = 6√2 cm ≈ 8.49 cm. Midpoint O of BD is center of circle passing through A and C. Radius = OA = distance from O to A = half diagonal = 6√2/2 = 4.24 cm.

questions.text: Given a parallelogram ABCD with AB = 7 cm, BC = 5 cm, and ∠A = 60°, construct it and find the length of diagonal AC.
questions.explanation: Construct AB = 7 cm and at A, construct ∠A = 60°. From B, draw BC = 5 cm. Complete parallelogram ABCD. Using the law of cosines, AC² = AB² + BC² - 2×AB×BC×cos(120°) = 49 + 25 + 2×7×5×0.5 = 74 + 35 = 109. So AC ≈ √109 ≈ 10.44 cm. However, since ∠A = 60°, diagonal AC is approximately 9.5 cm after construction.

questions.text: Construct a triangle ABC given base BC = 7 cm, base angle ∠B = 45°, and difference of sides AB and AC = 3 cm. Determine which side is longer and find the approximate lengths of AB and AC.

options.a: AB is longer; AB ≈ 5 cm, AC ≈ 2 cm
options.b: AC is longer; AC ≈ 5 cm, AB ≈ 2 cm
options.c: AB is longer; AB ≈ 6 cm, AC ≈ 3 cm
options.d: AC is longer; AC ≈ 6 cm, AB ≈ 3 cm

questions.explanation: Draw BC = 7 cm. At B, construct ∠B = 45°. Mark point D on ray BA such that BD = difference of sides = 3 cm. With center C and radius equal to BD, draw an arc intersecting BD at A. Since difference is AB - AC = 3 cm and AB < AC, AC is longer. Approximate lengths are AC ≈ 5 cm and AB ≈ 2 cm.

questions.text: Construct a triangle ABC given that base BC = 6 cm, angle B = 60°, and the sum of sides AB and AC is 10 cm. Write the steps of construction.
questions.correct_answer: Steps: 1) Draw BC = 6 cm. 2) At B, construct angle ABC = 60°. 3) With B as center, draw an arc of radius 10 cm cutting the ray of angle B at point D. 4) With C as center and radius CD, draw an arc intersecting the previous arc at A. 5) Join A to B and A to C to form triangle ABC.
accepted_forms: Construct triangle ABC with BC=6 cm, angle B=60°, AB+AC=10 cm by drawing BC, constructing angle at B, drawing arcs with radius 10 cm and CD to locate A, then joining A to B and C.
questions.explanation: Given base, base angle, and sum of other two sides, construction involves drawing base, constructing base angle, and using arcs to locate the vertex satisfying the sum condition.

questions.text: Construct a triangle PQR where base QR = 7 cm, angle Q = 45°, and the difference between sides PQ and PR is 3 cm. Describe the construction steps.
questions.correct_answer: Steps: 1) Draw QR = 7 cm. 2) At Q, construct angle Q = 45°. 3) With Q as center, draw an arc of radius (PR + 3) cm cutting the ray of angle Q at point S. 4) With R as center and radius RS, draw an arc intersecting the previous arc at P. 5) Join P to Q and P to R to complete triangle PQR.
accepted_forms: Draw QR=7 cm, construct angle 45° at Q, draw arcs with radius difference 3 cm to locate P, join P to Q and R.
questions.explanation: Using base, base angle, and difference of other two sides requires constructing base, base angle, and arcs representing difference to locate the vertex.

questions.text: Construct a triangle XYZ with perimeter 18 cm, base XY = 7 cm, and base angles X = 50° and Y = 60°. Provide the construction steps.
questions.correct_answer: Steps: 1) Draw base XY = 7 cm. 2) At X, construct angle 50°, and at Y, construct angle 60°. 3) Let the rays from X and Y intersect at Z. 4) Measure perimeter XYZ; if not 18 cm, adjust the position of Z by drawing arcs with radius (18 - XY)/2 from X and Y to locate Z such that perimeter is 18 cm. 5) Join XZ and YZ to complete the triangle.
accepted_forms: Draw XY=7 cm, construct angles 50° and 60° at X and Y, locate Z on the intersection of angle rays such that perimeter is 18 cm by adjusting Z using arcs, then join.
questions.explanation: Constructing triangle with given perimeter and base angles involves drawing base, constructing base angles, and adjusting vertex position so that sum of sides equals perimeter.

questions.text: Construct a parallelogram ABCD where AB = 5 cm and BC = 3 cm, and angle A = 60°. Write the steps for the construction.
questions.correct_answer: Steps: 1) Draw AB = 5 cm. 2) At A, construct angle 60°. 3) From A, draw a ray making 60° with AB. 4) With B as center, draw an arc of radius 3 cm. 5) From A, draw an arc of radius 3 cm cutting the ray at D. 6) Join B to C parallel to AD and D to C parallel to AB to complete parallelogram ABCD.
accepted_forms: Draw AB=5 cm, construct 60° at A, draw arcs with radius 3 cm from B and A to locate D, join points to form parallelogram.
questions.explanation: Parallelogram construction uses given sides and angle, constructing one side and adjacent angle, then drawing parallel lines to complete figure.

questions.text: Construct a rhombus with each side 4 cm and one diagonal 6 cm. Explain the steps of construction.
questions.correct_answer: Steps: 1) Draw diagonal AC = 6 cm. 2) Find midpoint O of AC. 3) At O, construct a perpendicular bisector to AC. 4) With O as center and radius 4 cm (side length), draw arcs intersecting perpendicular bisector at points B and D. 5) Join A to B, B to C, C to D, and D to A to form rhombus ABCD.
accepted_forms: Draw diagonal 6 cm, find midpoint, draw perpendicular bisector, mark points 4 cm from midpoint on bisector, join vertices to form rhombus.
questions.explanation: Rhombus construction with side and diagonal uses diagonal as base, perpendicular bisector, and arcs to locate other vertices.

questions.text: Construct a rectangle ABCD with length AB = 8 cm and width BC = 5 cm using ruler and compass only. List the construction steps.
questions.correct_answer: Steps: 1) Draw AB = 8 cm. 2) At A and B, construct right angles. 3) From A and B, draw arcs of radius 5 cm intersecting the perpendiculars at points D and C respectively. 4) Join C to D to complete rectangle ABCD.
accepted_forms: Draw AB=8 cm, construct 90° angles at A and B, draw arcs 5 cm from A and B to locate D and C, join points to form rectangle.
questions.explanation: Rectangle construction involves drawing base, constructing right angles at ends, and marking width using arcs to locate other vertices.

questions.text: Construct a square of side 6 cm using ruler and compass only. Provide the detailed steps.
questions.correct_answer: Steps: 1) Draw AB = 6 cm. 2) At A and B, construct perpendiculars to AB. 3) From A and B, draw arcs of radius 6 cm intersecting the perpendiculars at points D and C respectively. 4) Join C to D and D to A to complete square ABCD.
accepted_forms: Draw AB=6 cm, construct perpendiculars at A and B, draw arcs 6 cm from A and B to locate D and C, join points to form square.
questions.explanation: Square construction requires equal sides and right angles, constructed by drawing base, perpendiculars, and arcs to mark equal sides.

questions.text: Using ruler and compass, construct the locus of points equidistant from two given points P and Q. Describe the construction steps.
questions.correct_answer: Steps: 1) Draw line segment PQ. 2) Find midpoint M of PQ by constructing perpendicular bisector. 3) Draw the perpendicular bisector of PQ, which is the locus of points equidistant from P and Q.
accepted_forms: Draw PQ, construct perpendicular bisector by finding midpoint and erecting perpendicular, the bisector is the locus.
questions.explanation: The locus of points equidistant from two points is the perpendicular bisector of the segment joining them.

questions.text: Construct a triangle LMN given base MN = 5 cm, angle M = 40°, and perimeter LM + LN + MN = 15 cm. Explain the construction steps.
questions.correct_answer: Steps: 1) Draw base MN = 5 cm. 2) At M, construct angle 40°. 3) With M as center, draw an arc of radius (perimeter - MN) = 10 cm cutting the ray of angle M at point P. 4) With N as center and radius NP, draw an arc intersecting the previous arc at L. 5) Join L to M and L to N to complete triangle LMN.
accepted_forms: Draw MN=5 cm, construct 40° at M, draw arcs with radius 10 cm and NP to locate L, join points to form triangle.
questions.explanation: Triangle construction with given base, base angle, and perimeter involves arcs representing sum of sides to locate third vertex.

questions.text: Construct a quadrilateral ABCD where AB = 6 cm, BC = 4 cm, CD = 5 cm, DA = 3 cm, and angle A = 90°. Provide the construction steps.
questions.correct_answer: Steps: 1) Draw AB = 6 cm. 2) At A, construct angle 90°. 3) From A, draw ray AD = 3 cm on the constructed angle. 4) With B as center, draw an arc of radius 4 cm. 5) With D as center, draw an arc of radius 5 cm intersecting the previous arc at C. 6) Join B to C and C to D to complete quadrilateral ABCD.
accepted_forms: Draw AB=6 cm, construct 90° at A, draw AD=3 cm, draw arcs 4 cm from B and 5 cm from D to locate C, join points to form quadrilateral.
questions.explanation: Quadrilateral construction with given sides and angle involves drawing base, constructing angle, marking adjacent side, and using arcs to locate remaining vertex.

questions.case_text: Ravi is an architect designing a triangular garden in a city park in Mumbai. He needs to construct a triangle with a base of 12 m, one base angle of 45°, and the sum of the other two sides as 20 m. He plans to use only a ruler and compass for the construction.

subparts.text: What is the first step Ravi should take to construct the triangle?

options.a: Draw the base of 12 m and construct a 45° angle at one end.
options.b: Draw the sum of two sides as 20 m as the base.
options.c: Construct the perpendicular bisector of the base first.
options.d: Draw a circle with radius 20 m from one end of the base.

subparts.explanation: The first step is to draw the base of 12 m and then construct the given base angle of 45° at one end, as this sets the foundation for further construction.

subparts.text: After drawing the base and base angle, what should Ravi do next to locate the third vertex?

options.a: Draw a circle with radius 12 m from the other end of the base.
options.b: Draw an arc with radius 20 m from the vertex of the base angle to intersect the line.
options.c: Draw an arc with radius 20 - 12 = 8 m from the other end of the base.
options.d: Draw an arc with radius 20 m from the other end of the base to intersect the line from the base angle.

subparts.explanation: Since the sum of the other two sides is 20 m, Ravi should draw an arc with radius 20 m from the other end of the base to intersect the ray drawn at 45°, locating the third vertex.

subparts.text: Calculate the length of the other two sides of the triangle if the base is 12 m and the sum of the other two sides is 20 m.
subparts.explanation: Since the base is 12 m and sum of other two sides is 20 m, the other two sides could be 8 m and 12 m. The exact lengths depend on the construction but their sum must be 20 m.

questions.case_text: Anjali is designing a rectangular plot measuring 30 m by 20 m in her hometown of Pune. She wants to construct the rectangle using only a ruler and compass, starting with the base of 30 m and one base angle of 90°. She also wants to mark the locus of all points equidistant from two adjacent vertices of the rectangle.

subparts.text: What is the correct first step to construct the rectangle?

options.a: Draw the base of 30 m and construct a 90° angle at one end.
options.b: Draw the diagonal of length √(30² + 20²) m first.
options.c: Draw a square of side 20 m first.
options.d: Draw a circle with radius 30 m from one vertex.

subparts.explanation: The first step is to draw the base of 30 m and then construct a 90° angle at one end to start forming the rectangle.

subparts.text: How can Anjali mark the locus of points equidistant from two adjacent vertices A and B of the rectangle?

options.a: Draw the perpendicular bisector of the segment AB.
options.b: Draw the angle bisector of the angle at vertex A.
options.c: Draw a circle with center at A and radius AB.
options.d: Draw a line parallel to AB at half its length.

subparts.explanation: The locus of points equidistant from two points A and B is the perpendicular bisector of the segment AB.

subparts.text: Calculate the length of the diagonal of the rectangle with sides 30 m and 20 m.
